title: sprint.py production close #2 + widen SAFE_COMPONENTS by exactly one prefix
current_state: Assuming T-256 lands, sprint.py will have exactly one production close, on a SAFE component (scripts/, testing/, docs/, vault/). The guardrails still refuse ~80% of the repo surface - correct caution for run #1, but autonomy scope should be earned one notch at a time, not frozen forever.
target_state: A second production close on a different ticket, and SAFE_COMPONENTS widened by exactly one prefix - chosen from what T-256's run actually demonstrated (candidate: a single well-tested tools/ file like tools/tools_image.py, NOT agent/ or pi_agent.py which stay diff-first per PI.md section 10). The widening commit cites the two successful runs as evidence.
migration_plan: 1. Review T-256's run transcript in logs/sprint/: what did the runner handle well, where did it wobble.
2. Pick the one prefix to add based on that evidence; smallest defensible step (a single file path is a valid prefix).
3. Add the prefix to SAFE_COMPONENTS in scripts/sprint.py with a comment citing the two production runs.
4. Run close #2 on a verified ticket touching the newly-safe prefix: --dry-run rehearsal, then --auto-implement --max-cost 0.50, Ash gates the merge.
5. python scripts/verify.py PASS; testing/test_sprint_isolation.py must stay green (god-path refusal invariant untouched).
risk_notes:
Never widen by more than one prefix per cycle, and never add the risk-flagged set (pi_agent.py, agent/tools.py, agent/prompt.py, prompts/consciousness.txt, app/config.py, requirements.txt, memory/). If T-256 failed or wobbled, this ticket blocks until the diagnosis ticket closes.

2026-07-06: blocked on T-256 (see its notes) - not stalled by choice, there is simply no candidate ticket for either production close right now.
